zh.lifeSummary: Lady Edith Pierre於1325年出生在英國史洛普爾。她和Jeffrey Yonge至少有1個兒子。她去世於1364年在英國英格蘭享年39歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Edith Pierre于1325年出生在英国史洛普爾。她和Jeffrey Yonge至少有1个儿子。她去世于1364年在英国英格兰享年39岁。

nameMeanings.name: Pierre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jacques, Antoine, Yves, Michel, Jean Claude, Mireille, Jean Robert, Francois, Monique, Raoul, Serge.
French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian:

from the personal name Pierre, French form of Peter (see also below). This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Jean and Joseph ) and in Saint Martin. It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in Trinidad and Tobago, Saint Lucia, Grenada, and Bahamas).

variants.name: Perrault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Gisele, Jacques, Marcel, Roch, Alphonse, Andre, Donat, Emile, Francoise, Gaston, Ghislaine.

French: from a pet form of the Old French personal name Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter . Compare Paro , Parro , Parrow , Pearo , Perault , Pero , Perreault , Perro , Perrot , and Perrow .

History: Jacques Perrault/Perrot dit Villedaigre from Mons in Charente-Maritime, France, married Michelle Le Flot in Quebec City, QC, in 1654. Nicolas Perrault/Perrot from Darcey in Côte-d’Or married Madeleine Raclos in QC in 1671. Pierre Perrault from France married Geneviève Duclos in QC c. 1686. François Perrault from Cosne-Cours-sur-Loire in Nièvre married Jeanne-Suzanne Pagé in Quebec City, QC, in 1715. — In North America, this surname was originally (also) a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Louis Leparon dit Perrault (a son of Pierre Leparon from Derval in Loire-Atlantique, France), who married Marie-Josèphe Normandeau in Quebec City, QC, in 1751. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered forms Perrow and Pero) and also in the similar registers of the Huguenot Society of America and of the Huguenot Society of the Founders of Manakin in the Colony of Virginia. The Huguenot ancestor was Charles Perrault from Bordeaux in France, one of the settlers of Manakin, VA, in the early 1700s. However, his descendants bear only altered forms of the surname, such as Perrow and Perow.

variants.name: Perot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Antoine, Philippe, Remy.
French (Pérot): from a pet form of the Old French personal name Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter . Compare Perrow .
Altered form of French   Perret  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Lapierre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Lucien, Normand, Adrien, Alcide, Aristide, Arnaud, Julien, Pierre, Aime.
French, Walloon, and Flemish:

topographic name, with fused French feminine definite article la, for someone who lived on a patch of stony soil or by a large outcrop of rock, from Old French pierre ‘stone, rock’ (compare Pierre 2); or a habitational name from any of various places called Lapierre or La Pierre in France and Belgium (Wallonia), with the same meaning.

variants.name: Paret
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Philippe, Pierre.  Spanish Joaquin, Alberto, Caridad, Eduardo, Eugenio, Lourdes, Patricio, Wilfredo.
French: habitational name from (Le) Paret, the name of several places in various parts of France, ultimately derived from Latin paries, genitive parietis ‘wall’ (compare 3 below).
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Pierre  .

variants.name: Peregoy
meanings: Americanized form of French Périgord: habitational name from Périgord, the name of a former province in France, of Gaulish origin.

Alternatively, an Americanized form of northwestern French Périgois, which may possibly be a cognate of 1 above, or, more likely, a regional derivative of the Old French personal name Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter . Compare Pedigo , Perigo , Perrigo , and Pherigo .

History: The progenitor of the Peregoys was Joseph Peregoy, a Frenchman who came to Baltimore County, MD, in 1685. In North America his surname was first recorded as Peregois and later spelled in many other ways, including Perigo, Perigoe, and Perrigo. The majority of his descendants (through his son Henry) bear the surname Pedigo .

variants.name: Pearre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre.
Possibly also an altered form of French   Pierre  .
Altered form of English   Perry  .

variants.name: Peron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cecile, Fernand, Herve, Michel, Philippe, Virginie.
French (Péron): from a pet form of the Old French personal name Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter . Compare Perron 1 and 2.
Breton (Péron, rarely Le Péron): variant of Pédron (see Pedron ).

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

variants.name: Perret
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Emile, Jacques, Michel, Andree, Dominique, Etienne, Fernand, Henri, Solange.
French: from a pet form of the Old French personal name Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter .
English (Somerset, Gloucestershire and Wiltshire): variant of   Parrott  .

variants.name: Patriquin
meanings: Altered form of French Petrequin: from a pet form of Petre, a regional variant of the personal name Pierre ‘Peter’.

nameMeanings.name: Edith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English female personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + gӯð ‘strife’. This was borne by a daughter ( 961–984 ) of Edgar the Peaceful. (She was named in accordance with the common Old English practice of repeating name elements within a family.) She spent her short life in a convent, and is regarded as a saint.

variants.name: Edyth
meanings: Variant spelling of   Edith .

variants.name: Edythe
meanings: From an Old English female personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + gӯð ‘strife’. This was borne by a daughter ( 961–984 ) of Edgar the Peaceful. (She was named in accordance with the common Old English practice of repeating name elements within a family.) She spent her short life in a convent, and is regarded as a saint.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Edith Pierre was born in 1325, in Shropshire, England. She had at least 1 son with Jeffrey Yonge. She died in 1364, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 39.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Edith Pierre est née en 1325, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Jeffrey Yonge. Elle est décédée en 1364, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 39 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Edith Pierre wurde 1325 in Shropshire, England geboren. Sie hatte mit Jeffrey Yonge mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1364 im Alter von 39 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Edith Pierre è nata il nel 1325 nel Shropshire, England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Jeffrey Yonge. Morì nel 1364, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 39 anni.
